title: Review the post-change documentation risk in /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public: which user/operator-facing docs now contradict the new default assist=none path? Prioritize anything that could mislead operations or make tests/checks fail. Do not edit files; return concise findings with fi...
summary_text: Smallest high-value change: **emit incremental report checkpoints after each case finishes** (not only at final pass/fail). Why this is the best ROI: - `write_canary_report` already writes durable JSON+Markdown and `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` ([run-canary.sh:232](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/run-canary.sh:232), [run-canary.sh:296](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/run-canary.sh:296)). - Today it’s only called at plan-only/final paths ([run-canary.sh:638](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/run-canary.sh:638), [run-canary.sh:1089](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/run-canary.sh:1089), [run-canary.sh:1100](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/run-canary.sh:1100)), so long waits have no durable mid-run state. Minimal implementation: - Add `write_canary_report "in-progress"` once after each case verification block: - after regular block ([run-canary.sh:966](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/run-canary.sh:966)) - after alternate block ([run-canary.sh:1022](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/run-canary.sh:1022)) - after rollback block ([run-canary.sh:1084](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/run-canary.sh:1084)) Result: - If the run hangs/fails later, artifact upload still has partial `canary-report.json/.md`, and step summary shows what already passed/failed.
